exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "score": [0.9, 0.4]})
df = df.set_index("cliente_id")
print(df.index.tolist())
exercises.note: Hints set_index + index.tolist; demo S15-T1-B-DEMO pattern.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([0.9, 0.4], index=["C001", "C002"], name="score")
print(s["C002"])
exercises.note: Theory Series opening code example matches.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s1 = pd.Series([1.0, 2.0], index=["C001", "C002"])
s2 = pd.Series([3.0, 4.0], index=["C002", "C003"])
print(s1.add(s2, fill_value=0).sort_index())
exercises.note: Hints give add(fill_value=0) + sort_index; Series alignment taught via Index theory.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = "a,b\n1,NA\n2,3\n"
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(raw), na_values=["NA"])
print(int(df["b"].isna().sum()))
exercises.note: Hints + S15-T2-A-DEMO na_values pattern.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = "fecha,v\n2024-03-01,1\n2024-03-02,2\n"
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(raw), parse_dates=["fecha"])
print(str(df["fecha"].dtype))
exercises.note: Hints parse_dates + str(dtype); demo shows datetime64[ns].

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = "cliente_id,monto,z\nC001,1.0,9\nC002,2.0,8\n"
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(raw), usecols=["cliente_id", "monto"])
print(df.columns.tolist())
exercises.note: Theory usecols; hints verify z excluded.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "score": [0.4, 0.9]})
print(df.loc[df.score >= 0.5, "cliente_id"].tolist())
exercises.note: Hints loc score>=0.5 cliente_id.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [1.0, 2.0]})
out = df.assign(doble=df["score"] * 2)
print(out["doble"].tolist())
exercises.note: Theory assign; hints df.assign(doble=...).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
print(int(df.iloc[1, 0]))
exercises.note: Hints iloc[1,0] → 3.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9]})
df.loc[df.score < 0.5, "flag"] = "x"
print(df["flag"].fillna("").tolist())
exercises.note: Hints loc mask flag='x'; fillna('') for list.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9, 0.7]})
subset = df.loc[df.score > 0.5].copy()
subset["ok"] = True
print(subset["ok"].tolist())
exercises.note: Theory copy after filter; hints subset.loc...copy + ok=True.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [1.0, 2.0]})
c = df.copy()
c.iloc[0, 0] = 99
print(df["score"].tolist())
exercises.note: Hints c=df.copy(); mutate c; print original.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["lima", "Lima"]})
df["region"] = df["region"].str.title().astype("category")
print(df["region"].dtype.name)
exercises.note: Hints title then astype category; theory category dtype.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.to_numeric(["1", "a", "3"], errors="coerce")
print(s.tolist())
exercises.note: Theory to_numeric errors=; invalid → NaN.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-01", "no-fecha"], errors="coerce")
print(int(s.isna().sum()))
exercises.note: Hints isna sum; NaT counts as na.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"monto": ["1", "x"]})
before = int(df["monto"].isna().sum())
coerced = pd.to_numeric(df["monto"], errors="coerce")
after = int(coerced.isna().sum())
print(after - before)
exercises.note: Hints compare isna before/after; fixture expects 1 new NaN.
Hints if col not in columns; try/except KeyError print missing.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"]})
df["cliente_id"] = df["cliente_id"].astype("string")
print(str(df["cliente_id"].dtype))
exercises.note: Theory astype('string'); hints dtype string pandas.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
buf = StringIO()
df.to_csv(buf, index=False)
buf.seek(0)
rt = pd.read_csv(buf)
print(rt.columns.tolist())
exercises.note: S15-T4-B-DEMO export round-trip pattern.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import BytesIO
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1], "b": [2]})
buf = BytesIO()
df.to_excel(buf, index=False, engine="openpyxl")
print(len(buf.getvalue()) > 0)
exercises.note: Demo to_excel engine openpyxl; print True if bytes>0.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
contract = {c: str(df[c].dtype) for c in df.columns}
print(dict(sorted(contract.items())))
exercises.note: Demo contract comprehension; sorted keys.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0]})
mem = int(df.memory_usage(deep=True).sum())
print(mem, mem > 0)
exercises.note: Manifest demo uses memory_usage(deep=True).sum().

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0]})
manifest = {"rows": int(len(df)), "columns": df.columns.tolist()}
print(manifest["rows"])
exercises.note: Hints len + columns.tolist.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
import hashlib
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
blob = df.to_csv(index=False).encode("utf-8")
print(hashlib.sha1(blob).hexdigest()[:8])
exercises.note: Manifest demo sha1 hexdigest slice; hints [:8].
